reasoning: The process to ban a political party in Germany requires a ruling by the Federal Constitutional Court, which has exclusive authority over party bans under Article 21(4) of the Basic Law. The procedure requires immense evidentiary review, thousands of pages of dossiers, and extensive hearings. Historically, attempts to ban parties (such as the SRP, KPD, and NPD) have taken 2 to 4 years from the filing of the petition to the final ruling. Since the Bundestag motion to bring the proceedings was only put on the agenda at the end of January 2025, it is structurally and procedurally impossible for the Constitutional Court to complete a trial and officially announce a ban before July 1, 2025. Therefore, the probability of the ban occurring by this date is set to the minimum allowed value (2%).
